news.title: Spain Vs Argentina FIFA World Cup Final Shatters US Viewership Records
news.lede: The recent FIFA World Cup final between Spain and Argentina captivated nearly 63 million American viewers, shattering previous records and firmly establishing football's burgeoning popularity across the United States after decades of strategic efforts. Nearly 63 million Americans tuned in to watch Spain beat Argentina in the FIFA World Cup final on Sunday, shattering viewership records and underscoring football's growing appeal in the US after decades of efforts to broaden its audience. Held in New Jersey, the final remained scoreless until Spain broke through in the 106th minute to secure a 1-0 victory. Fox's English-language broadcast attracted 38.9 million viewers, while Comcast's Telemundo and its Peacock streaming platform pulled in another 23.9 million viewers for the Spanish-language coverage, the companies said.

news.title: World Cup becoming a 64-team tournament would create logistical challenges - so can Gianni Infantino's idea work?
news.lede: This year's World Cup is finally over - and after 48 teams took part, it was a long, long tournament. But just as we bid to say goodbye to the 2026 edition, this competition may be getting even bigger. FIFA president Gianni Infantino says any further expansion of the World Cup for 2030 - where six countries are set to host the tournament - ‌will be discussed now the 2026 World Cup has finished. And one solution FIFA are discussing is a 64-team tournament.

news.title: Spain crowned 2026 FIFA World Cup champions after defeating Argentina
news.lede: Spain claimed the 2026 FIFA World Cup title after super sub Ferran Torres fired Spain to their second men’s World Cup in a dramatic 1-0 victory over Argentina in a thrilling final that captivated football fans around the world. Both sides entered the match in outstanding form, having produced impressive performances throughout the tournament with Argentina chasing yet another World Cup crown. Ferran Torres scored the decisive goal early in the second period of extra time as Spain dominated the match from start to finish. Argentina struggled throughout the final, failing to register a single shot in 120 minutes.

news.title: ‘The scrutiny is constant’: referee Anthony Taylor retires from football
news.lede: The 47-year-old took charge of five World Cup matches. Taylor has refereed in the Premier League for 16 years. Anthony Taylor has announced his retirement from elite refereeing after a distinguished career spanning more than two decades and 831 matches. One of England’s most accomplished officials, the 47-year-old officiated 668 matches in the professional game in England.

news.title: ‘Reclaim football’: Sepp Blatter joins La Liga chief in call for Gianni Infantino to resign
news.lede: Blatter attacks successor as Fifa president. Javier Tebas says system is ‘rotten at its core’. The Fifa president, Gianni Infantino, has come in for heavy criticism from his predecessor, Sepp Blatter, and the La Liga president, Javier Tebas, with the pair united in the belief that he should stand down. As Spain celebrated their World Cup win on Sunday, Tebas accused Infantino of “destroying the football industry” by prioritising the World Cup over national competitions.

news.title: Could the World Cup be back in the US as soon as 2038? Absolutely
news.lede: Explosion in ticketing and hospitality revenue goes along with a Fifa desire to recoup lost TV income in future deals. As is often the case, it has been left to Donald Trump to say the quiet bit out loud. “We have to do this again and we have to do it while I’m around, you hear that Gianni?” the US president told Fox News in an interview broadcast in the buildup to the World Cup final.

news.title: Five players poised to make big transfers after the World Cup
news.lede: Besides the already established football stars who were expected to deliver great performances in the World Cup, the tournament also gave some other players the opportunity to break through and showcase their talents in the world’s greatest stage, getting the attention of European powerhouses. With the tournament already over, clubs will now focus their attention on bolstering their squads during the current transfer window in anticipation to the start of the domestic leagues. Here are five players expected to get big transfers thanks to their World Cup performances. Ayyoub Bouaddi (Morocco) The 18-year-old midfielder ended up his World Cup performance with an astounding 90% of his passes completed according to Sofascore.
